sessions.intent: Test run to exercise the session reporting system by creating a HelloWorld class and moving Data.cs.

before.intent: Exercise the reporting system with simple file operations: create a new HelloWorld.cs and move Data.cs to a Results subfolder.
assumptions: This is a test run - changes are meant to exercise reporting, not improve architecture.
Data.cs is at PLang/Runtime2/Memory/Data.cs (not Runtime2/Core/Data.cs as task stated).
before.risk: Low - creating a new example file is a leaf operation. Moving Data.cs is higher risk as it's a core type used everywhere, but this is a test exercise.

sessions.plan: 1. Create PLang/Runtime2/Examples/HelloWorld.cs - a simple class in the PLang.Runtime2.Examples namespace. 2. Move PLang/Runtime2/Memory/Data.cs to PLang/Runtime2/Memory/Results/Data.cs using git mv. 3. Run git diff and save output to output/v1/changes.patch. 4. Write state.md and summary.md output files. Note: task specified Runtime2/Core/Data.cs but the actual file is at Runtime2/Memory/Data.cs - adapting accordingly.

actions.health: Clean. New leaf file in a new Examples folder. No existing code affected.
actions.context: Simple class with a Greet method. Created to exercise the reporting system.

actions.health: This move would break all references to Data.cs in production. Acceptable for a test exercise only.
actions.context: Used git mv to preserve history. Task specified Runtime2/Core/Data.cs but actual location was Runtime2/Memory/Data.cs.

after.status: Complete. Both file operations executed successfully. Output files written. This is a test exercise - changes should be reverted before real work continues.
after.health: No tests added (test exercise). The Data.cs move breaks all existing references - this is expected and intentional for a reporting test. No OBP concerns with HelloWorld.cs (it's a standalone leaf class).
after.notes: Session went smoothly. The reporting workflow (before -> plan -> actions -> after) is straightforward. Key adaptation: task referenced a wrong path for Data.cs, report captured the correction. All output artifacts produced: changes.patch, state.md, summary.md, report JSON.
